item.title: Rupee Plunge Sees India Turn to 2013 Taper Tantrum Playbook
item.description: India’s central bank may need to draw on its 2013 taper tantrum playbook and earlier balance-of-payments crises to mount an effective defense of the beleaguered rupee.

item.title: Traders Bet on RBA Pause With Rise in Curve-Steepening Trades
item.description: Australia’s weak employment report has sparked an increase in curve-steepening trades as investors bet the central bank is nearing the end of its interest-rate hiking cycle, according to traders.

item.title: Indonesia to ‘Listen to Market’ on After Shock Commodity Plan
item.description: Indonesia is “listening very closely to the market” as it builds a body to oversee key commodity exports, according to Pandu Sjahrir, chief investment officer of sovereign wealth fund Danantara, after a shock announcement that sowed confusion among traders and producers this week.

item.title: Nintendo Seeks to Top Conservative Switch Forecast by About 20%
item.description: Nintendo Co. has asked partners and suppliers to assemble about 20 million Switch 2 consoles in the year through March, roughly 20% more than the public sales outlook it issued earlier this month, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Japan’s Extra Budget Not Far From $19 Billion, Katayama Suggests
item.description: Japan’s extra budget will likely be in the region of $19 billion, Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama suggested while playing down the likely impact of the extra spending on government finances.

item.title: Metals Advance as Traders Track Progress in US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Copper advanced with other base metals as optimism the US and Iran can reach a peace agreement supported risk assets.

item.title: DeepSeek Founder Declares AGI Goal as $10 Billion Round Advances
item.description: DeepSeek’s senior management has told potential investors in its ongoing 70 billion yuan ($10 billion) funding round that the startup will prioritize groundbreaking AI research over short-term commercialization, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: China Rallies Asia-Pacific Against Protectionism at Trade Meet
item.description: China called for Asia-Pacific economies to deepen cooperation in the face of global tensions, as some of Beijing’s trading partners plan tougher measures to address their growing trade imbalance with the manufacturing powerhouse.

item.title: Nifty’s Technical Setup Improves While Macro Risks Persist
item.description: Traders are positioning for upside ahead of month-end expiry in Nifty contracts.

item.title: Renault Joins Foreign Firm Yen Bond Sale Rush With Samurai Sale
item.description: Renault SA priced its second-largest Samurai bond offering ever, as heightened volatility in government bond markets fueled investor appetite for relatively higher-yielding but stable assets from companies like the French automaker.

item.title: South Korea to Trim Long-Term Bond Sales as Part of June Cut
item.description: South Korea will mainly cut issuance of longer-dated tenor notes for its planned reduction in June bond sales, according to a top finance ministry official.

item.title: Philippines to Sell Up to 30B Pesos Treasury Bond Due July 2030
item.description: The Philippines Bureau of the Treasury in Manila said it will sell up to 30 billion pesos ($487.4 million) treasury bonds on May 26.

item.title: Lenovo Shares Surge 13% After Earnings Show AI-Driven Growth
item.description: Lenovo Group Ltd. shares jumped to the highest in 26 years on Friday after reporting strong growth in AI-related earnings that offset difficulties from rising component prices.

item.title: FountainVest Is Said to Mull Sale of Swiss Micro-Tools Maker SPT
item.description: FountainVest is considering selling Small Precision Tools, a Swiss manufacturer of high-precision components and tools, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: Kawasaki Heavy Shares Rally on AI Tie-Up Plan With Nvidia
item.description: Kawasaki Heavy Industries Ltd. shares rally as much as 12%, the most since Feb. 9 on the company’s plan to collaborate with US tech company Nvidia Corp. and others on physical AI robot technology.

item.title: Philippine Peso at 63.50 Per Dollar Might Be Okay, Governor Says
item.description: The Philippine peso at 63.50 against dollar might be okay as long as the decline is measured and not inflationary, Governor Eli Remolona said.

item.title: Polymarket Is Said to Seek Japan Market Approval in Global Push
item.description: Polymarket has appointed a representative in Japan and is preparing to lobby for the authorization of prediction markets in the country, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: BofA Sees Foreign Exodus from Indian Stocks Extending Into 2027
item.description: Foreign selling in Indian equities may stretch into next year as Asia’s artificial-intelligence winners offer stronger earnings prospects at cheaper valuations, according to BofA Global Research.

item.title: Tycoon Plows Billions Into EVs as His Vietnam Stock Booms 1,000%
item.description: Vietnam’s richest person spent billions of dollars of his personal fortune on his emerging electric automaker and other ventures last year as the shares of his corporate group soared.

item.title: Tuas’ $1.1 Billion M1 Deal Lapsed Amid Singapore Regulator Probe
item.description: Tuas Ltd.’s planned $1.1 billion acquisition of Singapore major telecom operator M1 Ltd. has collapsed days after regulators halted their review of the transaction.

item.title: China’s National Team Set to Cut ETF Stakes by 90% in First Half
item.description: China’s so-called National Team is poised to cut its holdings of exchange-traded funds that track domestic equities by about 90% during the first half of 2026, according to an analysis by Bloomberg Intelligence.

item.title: Nidec CEO Says Data Center Demand Cushioning Blow From Scandals
item.description: Nidec Corp.’s recent financial performance is being bolstered by its data-center business, despite quality control and accounting problems at the supplier of electric motors, Chief Executive Officer Mitsuya Kishida said.

item.title: North Carolina Sues VinFast Over Delayed, Downsized Factory Plan
item.description: North Carolina sued Vietnamese electric vehicle maker VinFast Auto Ltd., alleging the company breached agreements tied to a planned electric vehicle and battery manufacturing facility, and seeking to reclaim the project site.

item.title: Yen Traders Brace for Intervention Risk as Monday Holidays Loom
item.description: Yen traders are on alert for the risk of intervention on Monday as holidays from London to New York reduce market liquidity.

item.title: Yield Curves May Steepen Further in Key Southeast Asian Markets
item.description: The yield gap between short- and long-dated bonds in several Southeast Asian countries may widen further, as elevated oil prices increase inflationary pressures and deepen fiscal strains.

item.title: Japan Inflation Eases to Four-Year Low, Complicating BOJ Hike
item.description: Japan’s key inflation gauge rose at the slowest pace in four years as the government continued to help ease the cost of living, creating difficult optics for the Bank of Japan to raise interest rates soon.

item.title: Investors Look Beyond TSMC as AI Boom Spreads to New Winners
item.description: After several years flying high as Asia’s best Nvidia Corp. proxy, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. is increasingly vying with other artificial intelligence stocks for investor attention.

item.title: Ross Raises Forecasts Amid Sales Surge, Record Same-Store Growth
item.description: Ross Stores Inc. raised its sales and profit guidance after first-quarter results surpassed consensus estimates, aided by strong customer traffic among younger shoppers and the highest same-store growth in the company’s history.

item.title: Only High-Earning Britons Are Defying the Energy Shock, GfK Says
item.description: Confidence among the lowest-earning Britons plunged in May and even those on average pay are digging into savings to meet everyday expenses as the Iran energy shock hits household budgets, a key survey found.

item.title: Zhipu, Minimax Seen Joining HK Tech Gauge, Luring More AI Bets
item.description: China’s hottest AI stocks may be among candidates for inclusion in Hong Kong stock gauges, opening access to trading links that may trigger billions of dollars in inflows.
